title: Suno recruitment ad points to more music-industry dealmaking - Music Ally
text:
Suno is still battling lawsuits from Universal Music Group and Sony Music Group, but the company’s latest recruitment ad points to its desire for more dealmaking with rightsholders.
The ad is for
a director, business development, music industry partnerships
at the AI-music company, complete with a $240k-$265k salary and equity (and, er,
an AI-generated song about it
that takes
plenty
of cues from ABBA. Paging Björn Ulvaeus…)
The role will “drive the strategy and execution of commercial relationships across record labels, music publishers, and other music industry players” according to the ad.
The new hire will report to chief commercial officer Jeremy Sirota,
who joined from Merlin in February
. Expertise across recorded music and publishing, extensive negotiation experience and “comfort operating without a playbook” are on Suno’s wishlist for the role.
Another recruitment ad, for
a product manager, artists & creators
, points to Suno’s strategy to build up the people who are finding an audience on its platform with their generated music.
“You will identify high-potential creators, accelerate their growth, and define how they build careers within Suno,” explains that ad.
“Partnering closely with Music Marketing and Partnerships, you will develop products and infrastructure that surface talent, drive discovery, and turn creative momentum into audience, community, and revenue.”
The fact that this role’s salary-band is $180k-$280k, with a higher ceiling than the biz-dev role, suggests the importance Suno sees in building up its creator community, alongside striking more deals with rightsholders.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
